stories.headline: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility

stories.headline: Ukraine says key Crimea rail bridge ‘no longer exists’ after drone strikes
stories.summary: Ukraine said it struck a major blow to Russia’s grip on the occupied Crimean peninsula, announcing the destruction of a key railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal in a series of strikes. The bridge is the “first one” to be eliminated, Kyiv’s troops said.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court OKs ExxonMobil lawsuit over Castro-era property seizure
stories.summary: The US Supreme Court made it easier for US companies to seek compensation from Cuba's government for assets seized.

stories.headline: A decade after Brexit, Britain weighs costs and gains as it searches for a new leader
stories.summary: Ten years to the day since Britain voted to leave the EU, the economic reckoning is neither the collapse its opponents feared nor the renaissance its champions promised, and the anniversary arrives with the country once again leaderless, after Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday.

stories.headline: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
stories.summary: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran’s Unfrozen Funds to Remain Under US Control
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said Iran will only be able to use funds released from frozen accounts to purchase food and medical supplies from the US, seeking to ease concerns about peace negotiations that both sides say are making progress.  Brett Bruen, President of the Global Situation Room and former White House Director of Global Engagement joined Balance of Power to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Tech Selloff Hits Markets | Open Interest 6/23/2026

Global markets get a reality check as tech stocks slide again, while SpaceX fights to defend its $2 trillion valuation after three straight days of losses. stories.summary: The penalties, issued in an attack where a police officer was shot, dwarfed those given to Jan. 6 rioters and appeared to signal that at least some courts will deal aggressively with ICE protesters.

stories.headline: Appeals Court Allows Trump to Resume Expedited Deportations Nationwide

stories.headline: US Natural Gas Drops on Cooler Outlooks, New Pipeline Capacity
stories.summary: US natural gas futures declined as forecasts shifted to show cooler weather in the coming weeks, suggesting a decline in demand for gas-fired electricity as consumers pull back on using air-conditioners. Kinder Morgan’s Gulf Coast Express pipeline expansion project also entered service, indicating more gas will be moving out of the oversupplied Permian Basin into surrounding regions instead of being flared.

stories.headline: Korea AI Rout Revives Scrutiny of $290 Billion Levered-ETF Boom

stories.headline: Sharif Assures Pezeshkian Pakistan Won’t Let Down Iran
stories.summary: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif assured the visiting Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian his country won’t let down Iran, as the two leaders met in Islamabad after the US-Iran truce that Pakistan helped mediate.

stories.headline: Europe’s heatwave drives electricity prices to new highs as demand soars
